items.title: Trump's deadline nears - with little indication Iran is on board
items.standfirst: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.
items.content: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

items.title: Trump threatens Iran with attacks on infrastructure unless deal is struck
items.standfirst: President Trump has set a deadline of Tuesday night for Iran to make a deal, or face escalated attacks. “The entire country can be taken out in one night,” President Trump said, “and that night might be tomorrow night.” NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.
items.content: President Trump has set a deadline of Tuesday night for Iran to make a deal, or face escalated attacks. “The entire country can be taken out in one night,” President Trump said, “and that night might be tomorrow night.” NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

items.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
items.standfirst: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.
items.content: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

items.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz is a "very big priority" amid ultimatum to Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges if it doesn't make a deal to end the war by Tuesday is looming over a Pakistani ceasefire push.
items.content: President Trump's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges if it doesn't make a deal to end the war by Tuesday is looming over a Pakistani ceasefire push.

items.title: Trump says Iran could be 'taken out' on Tuesday
items.standfirst: U.S. President ​Donald Trump on Monday told reporters that Iran could be taken out in one night, "and that night might be tomorrow night," warning Tehran it had to make a deal by Tuesday night or face wider bombing raids. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.
items.content: U.S. President ​Donald Trump on Monday told reporters that Iran could be taken out in one night, "and that night might be tomorrow night," warning Tehran it had to make a deal by Tuesday night or face wider bombing raids. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

items.title: Why is Hormuz closure driving oil prices? #world
items.standfirst: Oil prices are reacting to the risk that the Strait of Hormuz —a narrow chokepoint for Middle East energy exports—may not function normally during the U.S. Israeli war with Iran. In the stories, the Strait is repeatedly linked to both threats and market expectations: if Iran does not reopen…

When the strait is threatened, closed, or obstructed, traders treat it as a potential disruption to supply—pushing crude prices…

clusters.title: Artemis II crew back in contact after looping around moon's far side
clusters.neutral_headline: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth
bullet_summary: Kristin Fisher, daughter of two former NASA astronauts and founder of Endless Void Studios, joins CBS News to discuss the milestone
CBS News' Bill Harwood and Mark Strassmann reported on the moment and Ambre Rose Trujillo, spokesperson for the Planetary Society, joined CBS News with her reaction

items.title: Artemis II crew back in contact after looping around moon's far side
items.standfirst: Artemis II astronauts are making history as they travel farther from Earth than any humans in history and conduct a moon flyby.
items.content: Artemis II astronauts are making history as they travel farther from Earth than any humans in history and conduct a moon flyby.

items.title: Artemis II sets new record as astronauts travel farther from Earth than ever before
items.standfirst: Four astronauts become Earth’s farthest travelled and exceed a 1970 record on the fifth day of the missionSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxArtemis II astronauts broke Apollo 13’s distance record at 1.57pm eastern time on Monday, hugging each other in the cramped capsule as they made history for being the first four humans to travel the farthest from Earth than anyone before them.Before hitting the record, the quartet dimmed the lights in their capsule and positioned themselves by the windows in preparation to set the long-distance record as they fly by the moon without stopping – with plans to ultimately swing around for planet Earth. Continue reading...
items.content: Four astronauts become Earth’s farthest travelled and exceed a 1970 record on the fifth day of the missionSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxArtemis II astronauts broke Apollo 13’s distance record at 1.57pm eastern time on Monday, hugging each other in the cramped capsule as they made history for being the first four humans to travel the farthest from Earth than anyone before them.Before hitting the record, the quartet dimmed the lights in their capsule and positioned themselves by the windows in preparation to set the long-distance record as they fly by the moon without stopping – with plans to ultimately swing around for planet Earth. Continue reading...

items.title: Artemis II crew names moon crater after commander's late wife
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew proposed new names for two lunar craters on Monday as they continued their spaceflight. One of the names was Carroll, after the late wife of commander Reid Wiseman. Retired NASA astronaut Cady Coleman joins CBS News with her reaction.
items.content: The Artemis II crew proposed new names for two lunar craters on Monday as they continued their spaceflight. One of the names was Carroll, after the late wife of commander Reid Wiseman. Retired NASA astronaut Cady Coleman joins CBS News with her reaction.

items.title: Artemis II crew loses contact with Earth as they fly behind moon
items.standfirst: The Artemis II has entered its scheduled communications blackout as the crew loops around the far side of the moon. Former NASA astronaut Ron Garan joins CBS News to discuss.
items.content: The Artemis II has entered its scheduled communications blackout as the crew loops around the far side of the moon. Former NASA astronaut Ron Garan joins CBS News to discuss.

items.title: Astronomer discusses new details Artemis II crew revealed about far side of moon
items.standfirst: Astronauts aboard the Artemis II broke the record Monday for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth. Franklin Institute chief astronomer Derrick Pitts joins CBS News with analysis.
items.content: Astronauts aboard the Artemis II broke the record Monday for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth. Franklin Institute chief astronomer Derrick Pitts joins CBS News with analysis.

items.title: Retired NASA astronaut says Artemis II crew is "bringing all of us with them" after breaking record
items.standfirst: Artemis II set a new record for human space flight on Monday, flying farther away from Earth than any crewed vessel has in the past. Retired NASA astronaut Cady Coleman joins "The Takeout" with her reaction.
items.content: Artemis II set a new record for human space flight on Monday, flying farther away from Earth than any crewed vessel has in the past. Retired NASA astronaut Cady Coleman joins "The Takeout" with her reaction.

items.title: Artemis II prepares to loop around far side of moon after breaking distance record
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew made history on Monday, breaking the record for traveling farther from Earth than any humans ever. CBS News senior national correspondent Mark Strassman reports.
items.content: The Artemis II crew made history on Monday, breaking the record for traveling farther from Earth than any humans ever. CBS News senior national correspondent Mark Strassman reports.

items.title: Daughter of two former NASA astronauts on growing up in a space family, Artemis II mission and more
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew made history on Monday as they set the record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth. Kristin Fisher, daughter of two former NASA astronauts and founder of Endless Void Studios, joins CBS News to discuss the milestone.
items.content: The Artemis II crew made history on Monday as they set the record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth. Kristin Fisher, daughter of two former NASA astronauts and founder of Endless Void Studios, joins CBS News to discuss the milestone.

items.title: Watch: Artemis II begins moon flyby
items.standfirst: The crew of the Artemis II began their lunar flyby Monday after breaking the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth. Watch coverage from NASA TV.
items.content: The crew of the Artemis II began their lunar flyby Monday after breaking the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth. Watch coverage from NASA TV.

items.title: What to know after Artemis II breaks distance record, prepares for loop around far side of moon
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew on Monday broke the record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth, a mark previously held by the crew of Apollo 13. CBS News' Bill Harwood and Mark Strassmann reported on the moment and Ambre Rose Trujillo, spokesperson for the Planetary Society, joined CBS News with her reaction.
items.content: The Artemis II crew on Monday broke the record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth, a mark previously held by the crew of Apollo 13. CBS News' Bill Harwood and Mark Strassmann reported on the moment and Ambre Rose Trujillo, spokesperson for the Planetary Society, joined CBS News with her reaction.

items.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth
items.standfirst: Mission's four astronauts break record set in 1970 by Apollo 13, which flew 400,171km from the planet.
items.content: Mission's four astronauts break record set in 1970 by Apollo 13, which flew 400,171km from the planet.

items.title: Follow live: Artemis II's final lunar flyby
items.standfirst: Artemis II set a new distance record on Monday, breaking the previous record set by the Apollo 13 mission, for the farthest spaceflight from Earth. During the lunar flyby, which will last for several hours, the Artemis II crew will study specific lunar sites and phenomena as part of a 10-goal plan by NASA.
items.content: Artemis II set a new distance record on Monday, breaking the previous record set by the Apollo 13 mission, for the farthest spaceflight from Earth. During the lunar flyby, which will last for several hours, the Artemis II crew will study specific lunar sites and phenomena as part of a 10-goal plan by NASA.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts race to set a new distance record from Earth
items.standfirst: With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye.
items.content: With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye.

items.title: The 40 minutes when the Artemis crew loses contact with the Earth
items.standfirst: As the astronauts pass behind the Moon they will experience a moment of silence and solitude as communication with the Earth is blocked.
items.content: As the astronauts pass behind the Moon they will experience a moment of silence and solitude as communication with the Earth is blocked.

items.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist to find source of second missing airman report
items.standfirst: Trump targets media to find ‘leaker’ who revealed that a US airman was missing after being shot down by Iranian forcesDonald Trump threatened to jail a journalist – or journalists – who reported that a second US airman was missing after being shot down by Iran on Friday in an effort to identify their source.The badly injured airman hid in a mountain crevice to avoid capture before being rescued by a US recovery team that received heavy fire. The US president announced on Sunday that the service member had been recovered. Continue reading...

clusters.title: 'Vladimir Putin absolutely nervous' about Hungary's election, expert says
clusters.neutral_headline: Hungary puts gas pipeline under military protection amid false-flag accusations
bullet_summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban has suggested explosives found near a pipeline supplying Russian gas to Hungary and Serbia was "an act of sabotage"
Reported by France24, Guardian World, Sky News World

items.title: 'Vladimir Putin absolutely nervous' about Hungary's election, expert says
items.standfirst: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Dr Marc Roscoe Loustau, Affiliated Fellow at the Central European University, says that "next to Viktor Orban, the person who stands to lose the most in case of a Fidesz party loss is Vladimir Putin". According to press reports quoting intelligence officials, the powerful explosives found near a Serbian gas pipeline to Hungary may be part of a "false flag" operation to influence the upcoming election.
items.content: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Dr Marc Roscoe Loustau, Affiliated Fellow at the Central European University, says that "next to Viktor Orban, the person who stands to lose the most in case of a Fidesz party loss is Vladimir Putin". According to press reports quoting intelligence officials, the powerful explosives found near a Serbian gas pipeline to Hungary may be part of a "false flag" operation to influence the upcoming election.

items.title: Hungary puts gas pipeline under military protection amid false-flag accusations
items.standfirst: Claims explosives found near pipeline come before election in which PM Viktor Orbán is trailing in most pollsHungary has placed the gas pipeline that straddles the Serbian border under military protection, its prime minister, Viktor Orbán, has said, as accusations of a false-flag operation continued to swirl before a crunch election at the weekend and an official visit on Tuesday from the US vice-president, JD Vance.Orbán travelled to Hungary’s southern border with Serbia on Monday, one day after Serbia said it had found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond. Continue reading...
items.content: Claims explosives found near pipeline come before election in which PM Viktor Orbán is trailing in most pollsHungary has placed the gas pipeline that straddles the Serbian border under military protection, its prime minister, Viktor Orbán, has said, as accusations of a false-flag operation continued to swirl before a crunch election at the weekend and an official visit on Tuesday from the US vice-president, JD Vance.Orbán travelled to Hungary’s southern border with Serbia on Monday, one day after Serbia said it had found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond. Continue reading...

items.title: Russia says 'highly likely' that Ukraine planted explosives near gas pipeline
items.standfirst: ​The Kremlin said on Monday that while there was not yet conclusive evidence it was highly likely that proof would be found showing Ukraine had planted explosives found near a gas ​pipeline ‌in Serbia which carries Russian gas to ⁠Hungary.
items.content: ​The Kremlin said on Monday that while there was not yet conclusive evidence it was highly likely that proof would be found showing Ukraine had planted explosives found near a gas ​pipeline ‌in Serbia which carries Russian gas to ⁠Hungary.

items.title: Hungarian PM faces ‘false flag’ claims after Serbia says explosives found near pipeline
items.standfirst: Incident prompts political scrutiny across Hungary as Viktor Orbán trails in polls before next Sunday’s electionSerbia has said it found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond, sparking claims by Hungary’s leading opposition candidate of a possible “false flag” operation aimed at influencing the country’s elections.On Sunday, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, said he had been informed by Serbia’s president, Aleksandar Vučić, of the discovery near an extension of the TurkStream pipeline, which transports Russian gas through the Balkans to central and eastern Europe. Continue reading...
items.content: Incident prompts political scrutiny across Hungary as Viktor Orbán trails in polls before next Sunday’s electionSerbia has said it found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond, sparking claims by Hungary’s leading opposition candidate of a possible “false flag” operation aimed at influencing the country’s elections.On Sunday, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, said he had been informed by Serbia’s president, Aleksandar Vučić, of the discovery near an extension of the TurkStream pipeline, which transports Russian gas through the Balkans to central and eastern Europe. Continue reading...

bullet_summary: Trump said Iran is negotiating "we think in good faith

items.title: What Trump's latest news conference revealed about state of Iran war
items.standfirst: President Trump held a news conference on Monday after the crew of a downed fighter jet was rescued from Iran. During the news conference, Mr. Trump said Iran is negotiating "we think in good faith." CBS News' Weijia Jiang and Aaron MacLean have the latest.
items.content: President Trump held a news conference on Monday after the crew of a downed fighter jet was rescued from Iran. During the news conference, Mr. Trump said Iran is negotiating "we think in good faith." CBS News' Weijia Jiang and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

items.title: Trump, top officials share new details of rescue of U.S. airmen from Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump and top national security officials shed new light on the daring rescues of two American airmen who were shot down over Iran last week.
items.content: President Trump and top national security officials shed new light on the daring rescues of two American airmen who were shot down over Iran last week.

items.title: Takeaways from Trump news conference on rescue of F-15 crew

items.content: President Trump on Monday shared new details on the rescue of two U.S. crew members whose F-15 fighter jet was shot down in Iran last week. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has more.

items.title: Watch: Trump's full news conference on Iran war, rescue of U.S. airmen | Special Report
items.standfirst: President Trump and senior officials from multiple departments on Monday held a news conference detailing the rescue of two U.S. crew members in Iran who had their fighter jet shot down last Friday. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report.
items.content: President Trump and senior officials from multiple departments on Monday held a news conference detailing the rescue of two U.S. crew members in Iran who had their fighter jet shot down last Friday. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report.

items.title: Second airman in F-15E that was shot down over Iran is rescued safely, U.S. officials say
items.standfirst: U.S. forces safely rescued the second F-15E crew member of a fighter jet that went down over Iran on Friday, two U.S. officials said late Saturday
items.content: U.S. forces safely rescued the second F-15E crew member of a fighter jet that went down over Iran on Friday, two U.S. officials said late Saturday

clusters.title: Waitrose under pressure to reinstate worker sacked after stopping shoplifter
clusters.neutral_headline: Waitrose under pressure to reinstate worker sacked after stopping shoplifter
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Waitrose under pressure to reinstate worker sacked after stopping shoplifter
items.standfirst: Retailer faces public outcry over treatment of Walker Smith, who tackled shoplifter stealing Easter eggs at London storeWaitrose is under growing pressure to reinstate an employee of 17 years who was sacked after tackling a shoplifter who was trying to steal Lindt Gold Bunny Easter eggs.The retailer has faced public outcry over its treatment of Walker Smith, who was fired two days after he stopped the shoplifter taking items from the Easter egg display. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Tories urge Waitrose to reinstate worker sacked 'after tackling shoplifter'
items.standfirst: The Waitrose employee says he was dismissed from his job after 17 years for trying to stop a theft of Easter eggs.
items.content: The Waitrose employee says he was dismissed from his job after 17 years for trying to stop a theft of Easter eggs.

items.title: What deal did Neurocrine make for Soleno? #health
items.standfirst: Neurocrine Biosciences to acquire Soleno Therapeutics Neurocrine Biosciences announced a $2.9 billion acquisition of Soleno Therapeutics, a company developing a treatment for Prader Willi syndrome. The deal structure centers on Neurocrine paying $53 per share for Soleno. The acquisition matters…
